title: Insalata Caprese

ingredients.name: fresh mozzarella or pizza mozzarella block, sliced 5 mm

subs.name: firm paneer, sliced thin and soaked in salted water for 10 minutes
subs.notes: pat completely dry before plating; it mimics the texture of fresh mozzarella well

subs.name: roasted red capsicum strips for a winter version
subs.notes: roast until blistered, peel, and layer just as you would fresh tomato

subs.name: fresh mint leaves or microgreens
subs.notes: mint gives a cooler, slightly different character; use sparingly

subs.name: cold-pressed coconut oil (liquid state) or avocado oil
subs.notes: use the best oil you have — this dish has nowhere to hide a poor ingredient

steps.text: Slice the tomatoes and mozzarella to roughly equal thickness — about 5 mm — so each bite has both in balance. Pat the mozzarella dry with kitchen paper if it is swimming in whey.
Arrange alternating overlapping slices of tomato and mozzarella in a single layer on a flat plate, slightly fanning each slice to one side so the colours alternate visibly.
Tuck whole basil leaves between and on top of the slices. Season generously with salt and a crack of black pepper.
Drizzle the olive oil in a thin, generous stream from a height so it falls evenly across the entire plate. Serve immediately — do not refrigerate once dressed, as cold kills the tomato flavour.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cheese → firm paneer, sliced thin and soaked in salted water for 10 minutes (pat completely dry before plating; it mimics the texture of fresh mozzarella well); basil → fresh mint leaves or microgreens (mint gives a cooler, slightly different character; use sparingly); olive-oil → cold-pressed coconut oil (liquid state) or avocado oil (use the best oil you have — this dish has nowhere to hide a poor ingredient); tomato → roasted red capsicum strips for a winter version (roast until blistered, peel, and layer just as you would fresh tomato)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Amul mozzarella blocks (refrigerator aisle) are widely available and work perfectly when sliced thin. Paneer is an excellent and genuinely flavourful substitute — press-dry it and soak in lightly salted water for 15 minutes for a milder, milkier result. Use only the best olive oil you have as there is nothing to mask an inferior one. Ripe desi or hybrid tomatoes from the market — especially the large Bangalore or Pune varieties — are ideal.
